response: solution The primary error in the erroneous code is likely a failure to handle duplicate values correctly when the most frequent element appears more than half the time, causing adjacent equal elements. solution To fix this, we must first sort the array and then interleave elements from the second half with the first half to ensure strict alternation. solution This approach guarantees that even with many duplicates, the pattern `nums[i] <= nums[i+1] >= nums[i+2]` is maintained without adjacent equals whenever mathematically possible. solution For the follow-up request of O(n) time without sorting, we can use a selection algorithm to find the median and then partition the array into elements greater than and less than the median. solution By placing the median and smaller elements in even indices and larger elements in odd indices, we achieve the desired wiggle sort in linear time. solution This method works seamlessly for both positive and negative integers since it relies only on relative comparisons rather than absolute values. solution The final implementation ensures robustness against edge cases like all identical elements or heavy skew in frequency distribution.
